ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_03301103 covers black tea beverage as a category; no specific Assam black tea entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers black tea generically; no Assam-specific or large-serving variant code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 227313005 covers black tea as a substance; no Assam-specific or beverage-serving variant code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of Assam black tea as served at KOI Thé, approximately 500 ml, containing brewed Assam black tea and water with no added milk or sugar in the base preparation.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 1104262)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for brewed black tea (prepared with tap water) used as the closest approved source for plain Assam black tea. Assam black tea brewed without milk or sugar has a nutritional profile essentially identical to generic brewed black tea. Values represent the plain brewed beverage per 100 g (approximately 100 ml). KOI Thé large-size serving is estimated at 500 ml; no added sugar or milk assumed for the base product.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; plain brewed black tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrate and is assigned effectively zero GI by convention
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed black tea without added sugar or milk contains approximately 0.3 g carbohydrate per 100 ml, all of which is non-glycemic. No direct GI test exists for plain Assam black tea. By convention for essentially carbohydrate-free beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because the carbohydrate content per serving is below the threshold for a meaningful glycemic load. Reference: Atkinson FS et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (3 mg per 100 ml); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Plain Assam black tea contains approximately 3 mg sodium per 100 ml and is suitable for individuals managing hypertension; however, patients should avoid adding sugar syrups or high-sodium flavour additives that may be offered as customisations at bubble-tea outlets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate and zero glycemic load in the unsweetened base preparation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Unsweetened Assam black tea has a glycemic load of effectively zero and does not raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should confirm that no sugar syrup or sweetened base is added, as KOI Thé customisations can substantially increase the sugar content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and presence of tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain Assam black tea contains no saturated fat or cholesterol and provides tea polyphenols associated with modest cardioprotective effects in epidemiological studies; the unsweetened form is appropriate for individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Assam Black Tea (Large) — known in Chinese as 大杯阿薩姆紅茶 — is a large-serving brewed black tea beverage sold at KOI Thé, a Taiwanese bubble-tea chain with outlets across Singapore. The tea is made from Assam black tea leaves sourced from the Assam region of northeastern India, steeped in hot water and typically served chilled over ice in a large cup of approximately 500 ml. In its base form without added sugar or milk, it is a plain black tea with a robust, malty, and slightly astringent flavour characteristic of Assam-origin teas. The beverage is extremely low in calories (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible protein and fat, and provides only trace carbohydrates (approximately 0.3 g per 100 ml), all of which are non-glycemic. It contains small amounts of potassium (37 mg per 100 ml) and folate (5 mcg DFE per 100 ml) as well as tea polyphenols including theaflavins and thearubigins, which have been associated with antioxidant and cardioprotective properties in observational research. The glycemic index is effectively zero and the glycemic load per large serving is 0.0, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease risk, provided no sugar syrup or sweetened additives are included. It is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free in its plain form. Customisation options at KOI Thé (e.g., sugar level, ice level, added toppings) can significantly alter the nutritional profile.
common_user_queries: Is KOI Assam black tea healthy?
How many calories in KOI large Assam black tea?
Is Assam black tea suitable for diabetics?
Does Assam black tea have sugar?
What is 大杯阿薩姆紅茶?
Assam black tea GI glycemic index
Calories in large black tea Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed black tea (FDC ID 1104262), which is the closest approved source for plain brewed Assam black tea. Assam black tea brewed without additives is nutritionally equivalent to generic brewed black tea. No Singapore-specific or Assam-specific entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact KOI Thé preparation (water temperature, steep time, leaf grade, serving size) is not independently verified against an approved primary source.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is 1.0 kcal per 100 ml — this is the naturally occurring trace energy from dissolved tea solids; it is not zero. 2. protein_g is naturally zero for plain brewed black tea as confirmed by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 1104262). 3. fat_g is naturally zero for plain brewed black tea; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. saturated_fat_g is naturally zero; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. sugar_g is naturally zero in unsweetened brewed black tea; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 6. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero in brewed black tea (tea leaves are not consumed); confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 7. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for essentially carbohydrate-free beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g per serving is below the meaningful threshold. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0 as confirmed by USDA source for brewed black tea. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as confirmed by USDA source. 10. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are 0.0 as reported by USDA FoodData Central for brewed black tea. 11. calcium_mg is 0.0 per USDA source. 12. Serving size of 500 ml (500 g) is estimated for KOI Thé large cup based on standard bubble-tea large-cup conventions; exact volume not independently verified. 13. Nutritional profile reflects the plain unsweetened base; KOI Thé customisations (sugar level, toppings) are not included. 14. The food_name 'Assam Black Tea (Large)' is used as the canonical English name; the input food_name 'L-Assam Black Tea' is preserved as an alias. 15. Taiwanese cuisine classification is retained from the input as KOI Thé is a Taiwanese brand; the tea leaf origin is Assam, India.
